oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Another Record-Breaking Breeding Season for the Blue-throated Macaw
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Paterson, Dorothy
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: The 2025 breeding season marked a historic milestone: 19 Blue-throated Macaws successfully fledged from the Nido Adoptivo nest boxes at the Laney Rickman Blue-throated Macaw Reserve in Bolivia-the highest number ever recorded in a single season since the project began. This achievement raises the total to 164 wild-fledged chicks, thanks to this targeted nest box initiative that continues to help pull the species back from the brink of extinction. Out of the 128 nest boxes installed for this breeding season, 11 were active with Blue-throated Macaws: a total of 32 eggs were laid, 24 hatched, and 19 chicks fledged. The first egg was found on December 16, 2024, and the last chick fledged on June 1, 2025. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): Volume 52 July 2025 issue of AFA Watchbird
